_comment: Hand-written correctness cases (the only hand-written fixtures). Each carries `expect_output`; the fixture declares `function main()` and the runner `cargo run`s it, comparing stdout. `console.log(x)` lowers to `println!("{}", x)` (Display, not Debug), so fixtures log primitives or joined strings — never a bare Vec/struct.

features.fixture: function main(): void { const xs: number[] = [1, 2, 3]; console.log(xs.join("-")); }

features.note: JSON.stringify(42) inlines serde_json::to_string (serde_json dep injected, no __ds helper); prints 42

features.fixture: function main(): void { let m: Map<string, number> = new Map(); m.set("a", 1); console.log(m.size); console.log(m.has("a")); console.log(m.has("b")); }
features.note: Map<K,V> -> HashMap; set/size/has map to insert/len/contains_key

features.fixture: function main(): void { let s: Set<string> = new Set(); s.add("a"); s.add("b"); s.add("a"); console.log(s.size); console.log(s.has("b")); }
features.note: Set<T> -> HashSet<T: Hash+Eq>; string elements work (number can't — f64 has no Eq)
